name: Vehicle Boost
description: A temporary vehicle power state that converts a stored meter, pickup, drift reward, or scripted trigger into extra acceleration and stronger audiovisual emphasis.
design_notes: Boost is a pacing lever for overtakes, recovery, shortcuts, and stunt rewards. It needs a clear resource rule and a predictable exit so the player can plan around it. The main design risk is turning every straightaway into mandatory boost use or letting chained boost states erase steering and collision tension.
design_purpose: Create short windows of high-intensity vehicle control.
Reward racing lines, pickups, drifting, drafting, or risk-taking with a visible payoff.
Let designers shape comeback moments without removing the need to steer well.
player_fantasy: The player feels a surge of power as the vehicle launches past rivals or clears a dangerous gap.

parameters.name: vehicle_id
parameters.description: Vehicle instance receiving the boost state.

parameters.name: boost_input
parameters.description: Activation source accepted by the boost controller.

parameters.name: boost_meter_id
parameters.description: Stored resource pool consumed or checked on activation.

parameters.name: boost_window_ms
parameters.description: Active burst window after a successful activation.

parameters.name: boost_acceleration_multiplier
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to drivetrain acceleration while boosting.

parameters.name: recharge_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule that grants meter after qualifying driving events.

parameters.name: overheat_state_id
parameters.description: Optional lockout state after excessive chained activation.

edge_cases: Boost activates while the vehicle is airborne and should not produce impossible steering correction.
A boost pickup is collected on the same server tick as meter activation.
The vehicle hits a wall during the burst and needs damage, camera, and meter state resolved in order.
Pause, replay rewind, or checkpoint restore occurs during an active boost_window_ms.
Client prediction starts boost locally but the server rejects the activation because meter was already spent.
common_bugs: Boost meter is deducted twice when activation and pickup collection arrive in the same update.
Camera field of view remains widened after interruption, respawn, or vehicle swap.
Boost acceleration ignores surface traction and makes ice, mud, and ramps behave like flat asphalt.
Predicted boost effects show on clients even after server reconciliation cancels the state.
Overheat lockout never clears because it uses paused UI clock instead of gameplay clock.
balancing_notes: Short bursts reward timing; long bursts become route-planning resources and need stronger counterplay from turns or hazards.
Recharge rules determine behavior: drift charging encourages risky cornering, pickup charging encourages track control, and passive recharge reduces pressure.
Boost should amplify skillful positioning rather than fully correcting poor exits from corners.
accessibility_notes: Offer reduced camera push, reduced shake, and lower audio spike settings for boost feedback.
Make boost meter state readable through shape, text, or placement rather than color alone.
Support hold, toggle, or auto-trigger variants when repeated trigger pressure is uncomfortable.

implementation_notes.unity: Track boost_meter_id, boost_window_ms, and overheat_state_id in the vehicle controller, then apply the acceleration multiplier inside FixedUpdate after traction limits are known. Netcode projects should send activation requests with client_input_sequence and reconcile rejected bursts without leaving camera or audio state active.
implementation_notes.godot: Use _physics_process for the burst solve and a separate Timer or gameplay clock field for boost_window_ms. Emit boost_started and boost_ended signals so camera FOV, particles, and UI subscribe to authoritative state changes instead of polling button input.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement boost as a Gameplay Ability or vehicle component state with replicated activation, prediction keys, and explicit end events. Apply drivetrain modifiers before Chaos vehicle output, and clear camera and audio cues on ability cancel, crash, respawn, or possession change.
implementation_notes.web: Process boost through a fixed-step reducer that consumes meter, writes boost_active_until_ms, and logs the source event. Phaser or Three.js rendering should read state for effects, while rollback snapshots include meter, active window, and overheat fields.

example_games.title: Mario Kart 8
example_games.note: Uses temporary speed boosts from drifts, items, and track features.

example_games.title: Rocket League
example_games.note: Uses a boost meter as a core vehicle movement resource.
